companies.name: NVIDIA
companies.summary: Kyber NVL144 — NVIDIA's next-generation rack system — has been delayed by more than 12 months to 2028, per SemiAnalysis reporting on July 6, due to PCB midplane manufacturing failures. The NVL576 configuration is also likely delayed; NVL72x2 back-to-back was canceled. The report sent Asian PCB stocks sliding. Vera Rubin production shipments to hyperscalers remain underway from July 2026. Jensen Huang is a founding member of the UN/ITU AI for Good Global Commission with inaugural meeting July 8 in Geneva. NVDA market cap ~$4.7 trillion; FY2026 revenue $215.9B.

items.headline: NVIDIA Kyber NVL144 rack system delayed to 2028 — PCB midplane manufacturing failures cited
items.detail: SemiAnalysis reported July 6 that NVIDIA's Kyber NVL144 rack architecture has slipped more than 12 months to 2028 because the 78-layer PCB midplane remains too difficult to manufacture at yield. The NVL576 configuration — intended to link eight racks via optical interconnects — is also likely delayed or limited to small volumes. The NVL72x2 back-to-back rack architecture was canceled entirely. Jensen Huang had showcased Kyber NVL144 at GTC in March 2026, making the delay just three months after the demo a significant setback. NVIDIA did not respond to CNBC's request for comment. Asian PCB stocks slid on the report. Vera Rubin NVL72 production deployments already underway at hyperscalers are not affected.

items.headline: Vera Rubin production shipments to hyperscalers underway in July 2026
items.detail: NVIDIA confirmed Vera Rubin is in full production with first hyperscaler shipments underway from July 2026. AWS, Google Cloud, Microsoft Azure, Oracle Cloud Infrastructure, CoreWeave, Lambda, Nebius, and Nscale are among the first deployers. Vera Rubin delivers 3.5x training performance over Blackwell Ultra, 5x inference performance, and 10x reduction in cost per inference token. The Kyber NVL144 delay does not affect the NVL72 rack already shipping.

items.headline: Jensen Huang joins UN/ITU AI for Good Global Commission — inaugural meeting July 8 in Geneva
items.detail: Jensen Huang is a founding member of the UN/ITU AI for Good Global Commission alongside Andy Jassy (Amazon), Brad Smith (Microsoft), Jack Clark (Anthropic), and Aidan Gomez (Cohere). The inaugural meeting is July 8 in Geneva during the ITU AI for Good Summit (July 7-10), which runs alongside the UN Global Dialogue on AI Governance (July 6-7). The commission targets the UN Summit of the Future in September 2026 and will develop shared standards around AI infrastructure, health, education, food security, and trust.

companies.name: Alphabet / Google DeepMind
companies.category: Foundation models, search, cloud, and AI research
companies.summary: Gemini 3.5 Pro remains unreleased as of July 6 — now six days past the July 4 Manifold Markets checkpoint (~50% odds) and more than a week past the June 30 window. Gemini 3.5 Flash is the strongest broadly available model. The UN Global Dialogue on AI Governance opened in Geneva today (July 6-7) — a key policy venue for Google alongside the ITU AI for Good Summit (July 7-10). GOOGL ~$357. DeepMind talent exodus risk continues with Shazeer at OpenAI and Jumper at Anthropic.

items.headline: Gemini 3.5 Pro still unreleased July 6 — six days past July 4 checkpoint; September iPhone launch risk widening
items.detail: Gemini 3.5 Pro remains in limited Vertex AI enterprise preview as of July 6, now six days past the July 4 Manifold Markets checkpoint that had ~50% odds of release. Google has confirmed a July launch target with approximate pricing of $2 input / $12 output per million tokens. Key features: 2M token context window, Deep Think reasoning mode, and coding/token-efficiency improvements. The continued delay extends risk for Apple's September iPhone launch, which depends on the rebuilt Gemini-powered Siri in iOS 27. Gemini 3.5 Flash remains the primary broadly available model.

items.headline: UN Global Dialogue on AI Governance opens in Geneva July 6-7 — Google among participating companies
items.detail: The first session of the UN Global Dialogue on AI Governance opened today in Geneva (July 6-7), bringing together all 193 UN member states alongside industry, civil society, and academia. The event runs concurrently with the WSIS Forum 2026 (July 6-10) and the ITU AI for Good Summit (July 7-10). A second session will follow in New York in May 2027. Google, alongside NVIDIA, Microsoft, Amazon, and Anthropic, has representation through the UN/ITU AI for Good Global Commission, with its inaugural meeting on July 8.

items.headline: DeepMind talent exodus: Shazeer at OpenAI, Jumper active at Anthropic — GOOGL still ~12% below May ATH
items.detail: Noam Shazeer — Gemini co-lead, VP Engineering, and Transformer paper co-author — departed DeepMind for OpenAI in June 2026. John Jumper (Nobel laureate, AlphaFold2 co-creator) is now active at Anthropic. Three additional senior researchers also departed (Jonas Adler, Alexander Pritzel, Arthur Conmy). GOOGL is trading at approximately $357, down ~12% from its May ATH of $408.61. The Gemini 3.5 Pro delay and talent losses remain the primary narrative risks for Google's AI model execution timeline.

companies.name: Microsoft
companies.category: Cloud AI, copilots, and enterprise software
companies.summary: Microsoft Frontier Co. ($2.5B, ~6,000 embedded engineers) is in its fifth day with inaugural partners Unilever, Novo Nordisk, LSEG, Land O'Lakes, and Accenture confirmed. Copilot Cowork usage-based billing ($0.01/Copilot Credit) is in its sixth day. Claude models in Foundry on NVIDIA GB300 Blackwell Ultra are GA since June 29, with a 40% GB300 capacity expansion planned by September. The White House is advancing voluntary frontier model release standards and an announcement is possible this week. Brad Smith is a founding member of the UN/ITU AI for Good Global Commission.

items.headline: Microsoft Frontier Co. day 5 — Unilever, Novo Nordisk, LSEG, Land O'Lakes, Accenture confirmed as inaugural partners
items.detail: Microsoft's $2.5 billion Frontier Company — launched July 2 with approximately 6,000 embedded engineers — confirmed its inaugural enterprise partner roster on July 2: Unilever, Novo Nordisk, London Stock Exchange Group, Land O'Lakes, and Accenture. Led by Rodrigo Kede Lima (former Microsoft Asia president), engineers operate as virtual employees for six-to-twelve-month engagements. The initiative mirrors Amazon's $1B FDE program (June 30) and OpenAI/Anthropic FDE ventures in an industry-wide race to embed AI talent with enterprise customers.

items.headline: White House advancing voluntary AI governance standards — announcement possible this week; August 1 benchmark deadline
items.detail: The White House is finalizing voluntary standards for frontier model releases with AI companies including Microsoft, Anthropic, OpenAI, and Google, with an announcement possible as soon as this week. The standards stem from the June 2 executive order 'Promoting Advanced Artificial Intelligence Innovation and Security,' which established a voluntary pre-release access framework where developers provide government access to covered frontier models for 30 days before public release. Deliverables are due by August 1, 2026. The NSA is involved in the classified benchmarking process.

items.headline: Copilot Cowork billing day 6; Claude in Foundry GA on GB300; Microsoft evaluating DeepSeek for cost reduction
items.detail: Microsoft Copilot Cowork usage-based billing at $0.01 per Copilot Credit entered its sixth day following July 1 activation. Microsoft is reportedly evaluating DeepSeek V4 as a cost-efficient model within Copilot Cowork alongside Anthropic and OpenAI models — scoped to some Cowork workloads, not core Foundry or flagship Copilot products. Claude models (Sonnet 4.6, Haiku 4.5, Opus 4.8) in Microsoft Foundry on NVIDIA GB300 Blackwell Ultra GPUs are generally available as of June 29, with a 40% GB300 capacity expansion planned by September.

companies.name: OpenAI
companies.summary: GPT-5.6 Sol, Terra, and Luna remain restricted to approximately 20 government-vetted organizations — day 10 of the limited preview. Broader access is expected July 10-17 (4-11 days away). OpenAI is deploying GPT-5.6 Sol on Cerebras wafer-scale hardware this month at up to 750 tokens per second. The Apple breach-of-contract dispute remains unresolved with outside counsel retained. ChatGPT global AI assistant market share is 46.4% — the first time below 50%. OpenAI IPO leaning 2027. The 5% U.S. government equity stake proposal remains conceptual.

items.headline: GPT-5.6 Sol/Terra/Luna day 10 — broader access expected July 10-17; Cerebras deployment imminent
items.detail: GPT-5.6 Sol ($5/$30 per million input/output tokens), Terra ($2.50/$15), and Luna ($1/$6) remain in government-staged limited preview, restricted to approximately 20 organizations under a White House executive order requiring staged deployment benchmarking. Sam Altman confirmed broader access around July 10-17. OpenAI is deploying GPT-5.6 Sol on Cerebras wafer-scale hardware this month at up to 750 tokens per second for select customers. Sol sets new state-of-the-art on Terminal-Bench 2.1 for command-line workflows requiring planning, iteration, and tool coordination.

items.headline: OpenAI IPO leaning 2027; 5% government equity stake still conceptual; 42-state AG probe ongoing
items.detail: OpenAI filed a confidential S-1 with the SEC in June 2026, with Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley, and JPMorgan leading. The company is leaning toward a 2027 listing — CFO Sarah Friar cited operational readiness, Stargate cash burn, and SpaceX IPO volatility. The proposed 5% U.S. government equity stake (modeled on Alaska's Permanent Fund, worth ~$42.6-50B at implied valuation) is described as 'conceptual' and could require an act of Congress. A coalition of 42 state AGs opened a formal probe on June 12. ChatGPT global AI assistant market share: 46.4% — first time below 50%, with Gemini at 27.7% and Claude at 10.3%.

items.headline: Apple breach-of-contract dispute unresolved — outside counsel retained; users shifting from tokenmaxxing to efficiency
items.detail: OpenAI retained outside counsel and continues weighing a formal breach-of-contract notice against Apple over the lack of prime ChatGPT placement in the rebuilt Siri AI powered by Google Gemini. No lawsuit has been filed. The iOS 27 Extensions framework (user choice of ChatGPT, Claude, or Gemini inside Siri) remains disabled pending EU DMA regulatory clarity. CNBC reported that users are shifting from heavy 'tokenmaxxing' behavior to efficiency-focused usage patterns, pressuring OpenAI and Anthropic's average revenue per user assumptions.

companies.name: Anthropic
companies.summary: Anthropic tightened enforcement against Chinese firms (Ant Financial, ByteDance) bypassing Claude restrictions via VPNs, cloud providers, and overseas subsidiaries — 25,000 fraudulent accounts and 28.8 million exchanges detected April-June. Fable 5 Bug Bounty on HackerOne is in day 3. Tesla's $200/week AI spending cap (Grok exempt) took effect today — employees reportedly prefer Claude over Grok. Claude Sonnet 5 remains the default model at introductory pricing. October 2026 Nasdaq IPO target intact; S-1 submitted June 1. Samsung 2nm chip talks at early-exploration stage.

items.headline: Anthropic tightens enforcement against Chinese firms bypassing Claude — 25,000 fraudulent accounts detected April-June
items.detail: Anthropic is tightening access controls after detecting approximately 25,000 fraudulent accounts that executed 28.8 million exchanges with Claude between April and June 2026, systematically bypassing its ban on entities more than 50% owned by Chinese, Russian, Iranian, or North Korean entities. Firms including Ant Financial gave staff Claude accounts tied to Singapore subsidiaries; ByteDance reimbursed engineers for personal Claude subscriptions bought with VPN access. Anthropic now monitors accounts for computer time zones and usage patterns to detect transfer-station behavior. Enforcement tightening follows the Digitimes Asia report of July 6.

items.headline: Tesla $200/week AI cap takes effect today — Grok exempt; employees reportedly prefer Claude
items.detail: Tesla's $200-per-week cap on employee spending on third-party AI tools took effect July 6, requiring manager sign-off above the threshold. xAI's Grok and Composer are explicitly exempt, reflecting Tesla's $2 billion investment in xAI's Series E. Multiple sources report that Grok is unpopular among Tesla engineers, many preferring Anthropic's Claude for software engineering and knowledge work. The cap applies to competing models from Anthropic, OpenAI, and Google. Some Tesla software engineers were consuming thousands of dollars per week in AI tool costs prior to the cap.

items.headline: Fable 5 Bug Bounty on HackerOne day 3 — first public AI vulnerability disclosure program for a frontier model
items.detail: Anthropic's public Bug Bounty program on HackerOne (launched July 4) is in day 3. Focused on cybersecurity jailbreak vulnerabilities in Fable 5 under controlled conditions, it is the first public cybersecurity vulnerability disclosure initiative for a frontier AI model with standardized severity scoring and financial rewards. The program builds on the Glasswing jailbreak severity framework co-developed with AWS, Microsoft, and Google, and is part of Anthropic's pre-IPO security and compliance narrative heading into the October 2026 Nasdaq target.

companies.name: Meta
companies.category: Open models, consumer AI, and infrastructure
companies.summary: Meta Watermelon reportedly matched GPT-5.5 on internal benchmarks per Alexandr Wang's July 2 town hall. Meta Compute cloud is positioning to undercut AWS/Azure pricing by 20-30% using MTIA chips. Samsung is also reportedly in talks to manufacture custom 2nm chips for Meta alongside Anthropic. Meta One subscription tiers ($7.99 Plus / $19.99 Premium) are being tested in Singapore, Guatemala, and Bolivia ahead of a U.S. rollout. CoreWeave's $21B take-or-pay contract through December 2032 remains intact despite Meta Compute cloud competition fears. META trading near $612.

items.headline: Meta Watermelon matched GPT-5.5 on internal benchmarks — Alexandr Wang tells employees at July 2 town hall
items.detail: Meta Superintelligence Labs chief Alexandr Wang told employees at a July 2 internal town hall that Meta's in-training Watermelon model has reached the performance level of OpenAI's GPT-5.5 on closely watched AI benchmarks. Watermelon uses an order of magnitude more compute than prior Meta models, backed by 600,000+ H100 GPUs with a 1M GPU cluster planned. Zuckerberg admitted Meta's AI bets have not materialized as quickly as anticipated. The exact benchmarks were not disclosed; no independent third-party evaluation has been published.

items.headline: Meta Compute cloud: 20-30% cheaper than AWS/Azure using MTIA chips; CoreWeave take-or-pay intact through 2032
items.detail: Meta Compute — announced July 1 — is positioning to undercut AWS and Azure pricing by 20-30% using Meta's custom MTIA AI accelerators, offering hosted Llama model API access and raw GPU compute. Digitimes reported July 3 that server supply chain contacts see no change to Meta's 2026 shipment commitments. CoreWeave's $21B Meta take-or-pay contract through December 2032 is protected — Meta cannot redirect spend without financial penalty. META 2026 capex guidance stands at $125-145 billion.

items.headline: Samsung in early-stage talks to produce 2nm chips for Meta alongside Anthropic; Meta One subscription testing
items.detail: Samsung is reportedly in early-stage talks to manufacture custom 2nm AI chips for Meta alongside parallel discussions with Anthropic. Meta's MTIA chips currently use Samsung foundry processes, providing a commercial foundation for expanded cooperation. Separately, Meta is testing Meta One paid AI subscription tiers — Plus at $7.99/month and Premium at $19.99/month — in initial markets ahead of a planned U.S. rollout, competing directly with ChatGPT Plus and Claude Pro (both $20/month).

companies.name: Amazon / AWS
companies.summary: AWS Secret Cloud for Industry (ASCI) is live with Northrop Grumman as the first partner, giving defense contractors classified cloud access. The $1B Forward Deployed Engineering program mirrors Microsoft Frontier Co. in the enterprise AI support race. Fable 5 is fully restored on Bedrock following the July 1 export lift. GPT-5.6 Sol is expected on Bedrock July 10-17. Project Rainier runs nearly 500,000 Trainium2 chips with full Claude traffic. Andy Jassy is a founding member of the UN/ITU AI for Good Global Commission — inaugural meeting July 8 in Geneva.

items.headline: AWS Secret Cloud for Industry live with Northrop Grumman — classified cloud for defense contractors
items.detail: Amazon Web Services launched AWS Secret Cloud for Industry (ASCI) — giving U.S. defense contractors classified cloud access for the first time. Northrop Grumman is the inaugural partner. ASCI was announced at AWS Summit Washington DC (June 30) alongside the $1B IC Accelerated Modernization Framework for the Intelligence Community and the $1B Forward Deployed Engineering program. CIA Director John Ratcliffe confirmed CIA is using ICAMF with LLMs helping analysts surface hidden intelligence.

items.headline: Full Fable 5 suite on Bedrock restored July 1 — GPT-5.6 Sol expected July 10-17; Bedrock multi-model roster growing
items.detail: Fable 5 and Mythos 5 export controls lifted July 1 restored the full Anthropic Claude suite on Amazon Bedrock. GPT-5.6 Sol is expected on Bedrock in the July 10-17 window following broader access. Grok 4.3 is live on Bedrock with tool calling and streaming. Bedrock AgentCore Web Search is generally available in US East for real-time web grounding. Project Rainier runs nearly half a million Trainium2 chips for Anthropic Claude workloads with 1M+ chips expected by year end under the expanded $100B+/10-year agreement.

items.headline: AWS $1B FDE program inspired by Palantir — Allen Institute, NBA, NFL among early clients
items.detail: Amazon's $1 billion Forward Deployed Engineering organization places thousands of AWS engineers on-site with federal and enterprise customers. Engagements run in approximately 45-day cycles with pods of five or six engineers. Early clients include the Allen Institute, the NBA, the NFL, and Ricoh. AWS's $1B comes entirely from Amazon's balance sheet — structurally different from OpenAI's $4B and Anthropic's $1.5B FDE joint ventures that included outside investors.

companies.name: xAI
companies.summary: Grok 4.5 (1.5T parameters, V9 architecture, Cursor training data integrated) private beta at SpaceX and Tesla is confirmed. A 2T parameter successor is in final training targeting late July. Grok 5 (6-10T parameters) is in development on Colossus 2. Tesla's $200/week AI spending cap (Grok explicitly exempt) takes effect today. SpaceX's $60B Cursor acquisition is on track for Q3 2026 close. Colossus 1 continues serving Anthropic at ~$1.25B/month; Colossus 2 serving Reflection AI at $150M/month since July 1. SPCX trading ~$157.54.

items.headline: Grok 4.5 private beta at SpaceX and Tesla — 1.5T params, V9; 2T model late July; Grok 5 in development
items.detail: Grok 4.5 — a 1.5 trillion parameter V9 architecture model trained in part on Cursor AI coding data — is running in private beta inside SpaceX and Tesla. Elon Musk claims near-Claude Opus 4.8 performance in internal evaluations; no independent benchmark has been published. A 2 trillion parameter successor is in final training with expected completion late July 2026. Grok 5, targeting 6-10 trillion parameters, is in development on the Colossus 2 cluster. xAI plans to release a new model trained from scratch every month for the remainder of 2026.

items.headline: Tesla AI cap (Grok exempt) in effect today — Colossus 2 serving Reflection AI; Cursor Q3 close on track
items.detail: Tesla's $200/week AI spending cap — with xAI Grok explicitly exempt — takes effect today July 6. Multiple sources report Grok is unpopular among Tesla engineers. SpaceX (SPCX) closed at $157.54 on July 3. Colossus 1 serves Anthropic at approximately $1.25B/month through May 2029; Colossus 2 began serving Reflection AI at $150M/month on July 1 ($6.3B through 2029), meaning both Colossus facilities are committed to third parties. The $60B all-stock Cursor acquisition is progressing toward Q3 2026 regulatory close.

companies.name: Broadcom
companies.category: AI networking, custom silicon, and infrastructure software
companies.summary: NVIDIA's Kyber NVL144 delay (reported July 6) does not directly affect Broadcom's Jalapeño custom silicon timeline — Jalapeño still targets H2 2026 Stargate deployment. Six confirmed custom-silicon customers: Google, Meta, Microsoft, Anthropic, OpenAI, and one undisclosed. Q3 AI semiconductor revenue guidance ~$16B; FY2027 AI semiconductor revenue target exceeds $100B. AVGO is down approximately 26% from its post-Q2 earnings high. The Apollo/Blackstone $35B AI XPV Platform enables 20GW+ of Broadcom XPU compute for Anthropic and OpenAI through 2028.

items.headline: Jalapeño ASIC targets H2 2026 Stargate deployment — 1.3 GW in 2027, 10 GW through 2029; NVIDIA delay does not affect timeline
items.detail: OpenAI and Broadcom's Jalapeño ASIC — unveiled June 24, developed from design to tape-out in nine months — targets H2 2026 deployment in Stargate data centers. OpenAI committed 1.3 gigawatts of Jalapeño compute for 2027 with a 10 GW agreement through 2029. The NVIDIA Kyber NVL144 delay announced July 6 does not affect Jalapeño production or deployment timelines — Jalapeño is an inference chip, not dependent on the NVL144 rack architecture. Broadcom CEO Hock Tan confirmed six custom-silicon customers: Google, Meta, Microsoft, Anthropic, OpenAI, and one undisclosed. AI semiconductor revenue is now approximately 49% of Broadcom's total.

items.headline: Q3 AI semiconductor revenue guidance ~$16B; FY2027 $100B+ target intact; AVGO down ~26% from high
items.detail: Broadcom guided for approximately $16 billion in AI semiconductor revenue in Q3 FY2026 — a 48% sequential increase from Q2's $10.8 billion (+143% YoY). Full-year FY2026 AI semiconductor revenue guidance: $56 billion. FY2027 AI semiconductor revenue target: more than $100 billion. AVGO has declined approximately 26% from its post-Q2 earnings high. The Apollo/Blackstone $35B AI XPV Platform (operational since June 9) enables 20GW+ of Broadcom XPU compute for Anthropic and OpenAI through 2028.

companies.name: Palantir
companies.summary: PLTR is trading at approximately $129 range (markets reopened today after July 4-5 holiday). The Palantir-NVIDIA Sovereign AI Operating System running Nemotron open-weight models on air-gapped Blackwell Ultra launched June 29 and drove PLTR to an all-time valuation high on July 1. CEO Alex Karp published a 9-point AI sovereignty manifesto calling frontier AI pricing 'effing insane.' Army NGC2 advancing to broad deployment. BofA reiterated Buy with a $255 price target. Full-year 2026 guidance raised to approximately 71% revenue growth. Pentagon dispute court docs remain unresolved.

items.headline: Palantir-NVIDIA Sovereign AI OS launched June 29 — PLTR at ~$129; markets reopen today; BofA $255 target
items.detail: The Palantir-NVIDIA Sovereign AI Operating System — running Nemotron open-weight models on air-gapped Blackwell Ultra for U.S. government agencies — launched June 29 and drove PLTR to an all-time valuation high on July 1. Markets closed July 4-5 are now reopening. PLTR closed at approximately $129.30 on July 3. President Trump's financial disclosure revealed he holds at least $1 million in Palantir shares. Bank of America reiterated Buy with a $255 price target, stating 'The market is moving from AI curiosity to AI consequence, and Palantir is the leader in this transition.'

items.headline: Karp publishes 9-point AI sovereignty manifesto — calls frontier AI pricing a 'wealth tax'; Zeta Global rebuilding on Foundry
items.detail: Palantir CEO Alex Karp called the AI industry 'effing insane' in a July 1 CNBC interview and published a 9-point AI sovereignty manifesto on X, positioning Palantir's AIP with NVIDIA Nemotron as the sovereign, lower-cost government alternative. Agencies own model weights and training data on their own hardware, avoiding per-token frontier API costs at scale. Zeta Global is rebuilding its Data Cloud on Palantir Foundry, deepening PLTR's enterprise AI partner ecosystem.

items.headline: Army NGC2 advancing to broad deployment; 2026 federal contract wins $11.3B+; Pentagon dispute court docs unresolved
items.detail: The U.S. Army selected Palantir Foundry as the cloud data layer for NGC2, moving from prototype to broad deployment. Major 2026 federal wins: $10B 10-year Army enterprise AI services; $1B 5-year DHS AI platform; $300M USDA deal. Q1 2026 revenue: $1.632B (+84.7% YoY); U.S. commercial revenue $595M (+133% YoY). Court documents released July 4 reveal an unresolved policy dispute between Dario Amodei and Pentagon Undersecretary Emil Michael over autonomous weapons and domestic surveillance limits affecting the Anthropic-Palantir-AWS pipeline. Maven Smart System retains DoD program-of-record designation.

companies.name: Apple
companies.summary: iOS 27 developer beta continues with Gemini-powered Siri on track for public beta mid-July and September GA. Gemini 3.5 Pro's continued delay (six days past July 4 checkpoint) maintains delivery risk for Apple's September iPhone launch. OpenAI retained outside counsel over the perceived breach. Apple is evaluating Mistral AI as an additional model option and has reportedly discussed acquiring either Mistral or Perplexity. The iOS 27 Extensions framework (user choice of ChatGPT, Claude, or Gemini inside Siri) remains disabled pending EU DMA clarity.

items.headline: iOS 27 developer beta active — Gemini-powered Siri targets public beta mid-July, GA September 2026
items.detail: iOS 27 developer beta has been running since WWDC June 8. The rebuilt Siri is powered by a custom Google Gemini model (~1.2 trillion parameters) through a three-tier routing system: on-device for fast queries, Private Cloud Compute for medium complexity, and Gemini for complex multi-step tasks. Apple pays approximately $1 billion per year for the Gemini license. Public beta is expected mid-July; general availability September 2026. A 'Search or Ask' gesture allows users to hand queries off to third-party chatbots including ChatGPT, Claude, or Gemini.

items.headline: Apple evaluating Mistral and discussed acquiring Mistral or Perplexity — multi-provider AI hedging strategy
items.detail: Apple is evaluating Mistral AI as an additional AI model option alongside Gemini, reflecting a multi-provider hedging approach across its 1.5B+ active device base. Reports indicate Apple executives have also discussed acquiring either Mistral AI (~€20B valuation) or Perplexity AI ($20B valuation), though no move has been made. The hedging strategy reflects both Gemini 3.5 Pro delivery risk and a desire for leverage in renegotiating Siri AI model pricing and exclusivity terms.

companies.name: AMD
companies.category: AI accelerators, CPUs, and data center silicon
companies.summary: AMD Advancing AI 2026 is 16 days away (July 22-23 at Moscone Center, San Francisco). NVIDIA's Kyber NVL144 delay removes a competing rack platform from the 2026-2027 market window, potentially benefiting AMD Helios rack timing. MI450 samples are shipping to OpenAI (6GW partnership) and Meta ($60B/6GW). Oracle deployed a 50,000-unit MI450 supercluster targeting Q3 2026. MI500 series (CDNA 6, 2nm, HBM4E) roadmap confirmed. Q2 2026 financial results due August 4.

items.headline: AMD Advancing AI 2026 on July 22-23 — MI450 formal launch, MI500 preview; NVIDIA Kyber delay opens competitive window
items.detail: AMD Advancing AI 2026 at Moscone Center in San Francisco on July 22-23 is 16 days away. CEO Lisa Su is expected to formally launch the MI450 series, preview EPYC Venice (Zen 6, up to 256 cores), confirm Helios rack-scale systems (3 AI exaflops per rack), and provide a first look at the MI500 series. The NVIDIA Kyber NVL144 delay to 2028 — reported July 6 — removes a key competing next-generation rack system from the 2026-2027 window, potentially improving AMD Helios rack market positioning. Q2 2026 financial results are due August 4.

items.headline: Oracle deploys 50,000-unit MI450 supercluster in Q3 2026 — first publicly available MI450-based cluster
items.detail: Oracle announced a 50,000-unit AMD Instinct MI450 GPU deployment for the first publicly available MI450-based AI supercluster, with initial deployment targeted for Q3 2026. The MI450 features 432GB HBM4 memory, 20TB/s bandwidth, and 40 PFLOPs FP4 per GPU. This joins MI450 commitments from OpenAI (1GW, H2 2026) and Meta (Helios rack shipments H2 2026). AMD CEO confirmed MI450 samples are shipping to lead customers OpenAI and Meta; demand has exceeded AMD's own internal 2027 projections.

companies.name: Perplexity AI
companies.summary: Perplexity Computer for Windows with a hybrid local-cloud inference orchestrator is targeting July 2026 — Mac has been GA since May 7. Windows version integrates with Microsoft Office apps and local files. Perplexity for Gov is live via GSA MAS IT at $0.25/agency. ARR: $500M (+335% YoY); 100M+ monthly active users; $20B valuation from May 2026. 2028 IPO target reaffirmed. Apple has reportedly discussed acquiring Perplexity. CoreWeave's multi-year inference partnership provides dedicated NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 clusters.

items.headline: Perplexity Computer for Windows imminent — hybrid local-cloud inference orchestrator; Office integration
items.detail: Perplexity Personal Computer for Windows — featuring a hybrid local-cloud inference orchestrator that automatically routes tasks between on-device processing and frontier cloud models — is targeting July 2026 GA following the Mac launch (GA May 7 for Pro users). The Windows version integrates with Microsoft Office apps (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook) and local files, compatible with Intel Core Ultra Series 3 and NVIDIA RTX Spark hardware. CoreWeave's multi-year inference partnership provides dedicated NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 clusters, with Rubin systems rolling in H2 2026.

items.headline: Perplexity for Gov live via GSA — $0.25/agency; 2028 IPO reaffirmed; Apple acquisition discussions reported
items.detail: Perplexity launched 'Perplexity for Gov' alongside the U.S. General Services Administration at $0.25 per agency for 18 months, with zero data usage protection and access to ChatGPT, Gemini, Grok, Claude, and Sonar models. FedRAMP compliance certification is underway. Apple executives have reportedly discussed acquiring Perplexity at its $20B valuation as part of a broader AI platform hedging strategy. CEO Aravind Srinivas reaffirmed a 2028 IPO target and independence from Anthropic and OpenAI timelines.

companies.name: CoreWeave
companies.summary: CRWV is trading around $82-83 (markets reopened today) — partially recovering from the 14% drop triggered by Bloomberg's July 1 Meta Compute cloud report. CoreWeave joined the Nasdaq-100 on June 22 (15 months post-IPO). Evercore ISI reiterated Outperform/$150 citing the $21B Meta take-or-pay contract through 2032. Full Anthropic Claude traffic is running on Vera Rubin NVL72 clusters after the July 1 Fable 5 restoration. Revenue backlog: $99.4B (+50% QoQ). FY2026 guidance: $12-13B. NVIDIA holds ~11% equity stake.

items.headline: CoreWeave joined Nasdaq-100 on June 22 — markets reopen today; recovery from Meta cloud fears continues
items.detail: CoreWeave joined the Nasdaq-100 Index prior to market open on June 22, 2026 (15 months post-IPO), replacing Charter Communications. CRWV dropped 14% on July 1 following Bloomberg's Meta Compute cloud report and is at approximately $82-83 as markets reopen after the July 4-5 holiday. Evercore ISI reiterated Outperform with a $150 price target, noting the $21B Meta contract has take-or-pay protections through December 2032. Digitimes confirmed July 3 that server supply chain contacts see no change to Meta's 2026 shipment commitments. 37-analyst Buy consensus at a $142.29 average target.

items.headline: Full Anthropic Claude traffic on Vera Rubin NVL72; backlog $99.4B; NVIDIA Kyber delay does not affect NVL72 deployments
items.detail: Fable 5's global restoration on July 1 cleared the 19-day Claude workload backlog on CoreWeave's Vera Rubin NVL72 clusters. Full Anthropic inference traffic is now running. The NVIDIA Kyber NVL144 delay reported July 6 does not affect CoreWeave's existing Vera Rubin NVL72 deployment pipeline. Revenue backlog: $99.4 billion (+50% QoQ). FY2026 revenue guidance: $12-13 billion with an $18-19 billion exit run rate. 2026 capex floor: $31 billion for Vera Rubin NVL72 buildout. Nine of the top ten AI model providers are CoreWeave customers.

companies.name: Mistral AI
companies.summary: Next model release is projected around July 9 (3 days away) based on the ~71-day release cadence. Mistral OCR 4 launched, turning document extraction into a full enterprise AI play. A new 10 MW inference facility in Les Ulis (Essonne) is scheduled to open in Q3 2026. €3B raise at €20B valuation discussions are ongoing — nearly double the September 2025 Series C. Apple has reportedly discussed acquiring Mistral. The 1.4 GW AI campus JV with Bpifrance, UAE fund MGX, and NVIDIA has construction beginning H2 2026. €1B 2026 revenue target.

items.headline: Next model release projected ~July 9 — 3 days away per ~71-day cadence; new Les Ulis inference facility Q3
items.detail: Based on Mistral's approximately 71-day release cadence, the next model is projected around July 9, 2026. Separately, Mistral is opening a new 10 MW facility in Les Ulis (Essonne) dedicated to inference operations, scheduled for Q3 2026. The 1.4 GW AI campus JV with Bpifrance, UAE sovereign fund MGX, and NVIDIA in the Paris region has construction beginning H2 2026. CEO Arthur Mensch targets €1 billion in 2026 revenue.

items.headline: Mistral OCR 4 and Vibe agent platform launched; €3B raise at €20B valuation discussions ongoing; Apple acquisition reported
items.detail: Mistral launched OCR 4, turning document extraction into a full enterprise AI play with structured output, table recognition, and Vibe agent platform integration. Industrial launch customers for Vibe include Airbus, BMW Group, and ASML. Mistral is in discussions to raise approximately €3 billion ($3.5B) at a potential valuation near €20 billion ($23.2B), per Bloomberg — nearly double the €11.7B Series C from September 2025. Apple has reportedly discussed acquiring Mistral at the ~€20B valuation as part of its multi-provider AI hedging strategy.

companies.name: Tesla
companies.category: Autonomous vehicles, robotics, and applied AI
companies.summary: Tesla's $200/week cap on employee AI tool spending takes effect today (July 6), with xAI Grok explicitly exempt. Employees reportedly prefer Claude over Grok for software engineering. Q2 2026 earnings are scheduled for July 22 following Q2 deliveries of 480,126 — the company's best Q2 ever, beating consensus by 18%. Optimus Gen 3 V3 production is targeting late July/August at Fremont. The Cybercab fleet is at ~60-100+ units across Austin, Dallas, Houston, and the SF Bay Area.

items.headline: Tesla $200/week AI spending cap takes effect today — Grok exempt; employees prefer Claude over Grok
items.detail: Tesla's $200-per-week cap on employee spending on third-party AI tools took effect today July 6, requiring manager sign-off above the threshold. xAI's Grok and Composer are explicitly exempt, reflecting Tesla's $2 billion investment in xAI's Series E in January 2026. Multiple sources report that Grok is unpopular among Tesla employees — many preferring Anthropic's Claude for software engineering and knowledge work. The cap applies to competing models from Anthropic, OpenAI, and Google. Some Tesla software engineers were consuming thousands of dollars per week in AI tool costs prior to the cap.

items.headline: Q2 earnings July 22 — 480,126 delivery beat (18% above consensus); Optimus Gen 3 V3 targeting late July
items.detail: Tesla Q2 2026 financial results are scheduled for July 22 at 4:30 PM CT. Q2 deliveries of 480,126 — reported July 2 — beat the company-compiled consensus of 406,024 by 18% and set a new Q2 record. TSLA fell 7.49% on July 2 on 'buy the rumor, sell the news' dynamics. Optimus Gen 3 V3 production is targeting late July/August 2026 at Fremont as the Model S/X line (ended May 2026) converts to robot assembly. V3 features 22-DoF hands, the AI5 chip, and Grok 4.5 voice AI integration.

ipoTracker.status: October 2026 Nasdaq IPO target intact; China enforcement crackdown; Fable 5 Bug Bounty day 3; Samsung chip talks early-stage; ARR $47B
ipoTracker.process: Anthropic confirmed its confidential draft Form S-1 submission to the SEC on June 1, 2026, targeting an October 2026 Nasdaq IPO led by Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan, and Morgan Stanley with an expected raise of $60B+. The $65B Series H closed at a $965B post-money valuation. ARR: $47B as of May 2026. Fable 5 Bug Bounty on HackerOne (launched July 4) is in day 3. Anthropic is tightening enforcement against Chinese firms bypassing access restrictions — 25,000 fraudulent accounts detected April-June. Early-stage Samsung 2nm chip talks signal intent to develop in-house silicon. Pentagon dispute court docs remain unresolved.
ipoTracker.note: October 2026 Nasdaq target remains the working assumption. China enforcement crackdown and Bug Bounty program are both pre-IPO compliance and security narratives.

latestArticle.title: Anthropic tightens controls against Chinese access to Claude — 25,000 fraudulent accounts detected

ipoTracker.status: IPO leaning 2027; GPT-5.6 broader access expected July 10-17; 42-state AG probe ongoing; ChatGPT market share 46.4%; user efficiency shift pressuring ARPU
ipoTracker.process: OpenAI filed a confidential S-1 with the SEC in June 2026, with Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley, and JPMorgan leading. The company is leaning toward a 2027 listing — CFO Sarah Friar cited operational readiness, Stargate cash burn, and SpaceX IPO volatility. The proposed 5% U.S. government equity stake (conceptual, could require an act of Congress) is worth ~$42.6-50B at implied valuation. A coalition of 42 state AGs opened a formal probe June 12. GPT-5.6 Sol, Terra, and Luna remain restricted; broader access expected July 10-17. ChatGPT global AI assistant market share: 46.4% — first time below 50%.
ipoTracker.confidence: Confirmed by company self-disclosure; equity proposal reported conceptual
ipoTracker.note: July 10-17 GPT-5.6 broader access is the next near-term positive catalyst. User efficiency shift (tokenmaxxing to efficiency) is a new risk factor for ARPU assumptions.

latestArticle.title: OpenAI and Anthropic face new AI reality as users shift from tokenmaxxing to efficiency

ipoTracker.status: SPCX ~$157.54 (July 3 close; markets reopen today); Grok 4.5 private beta; 2T model late July; Grok 5 in development; Tesla AI cap (Grok exempt) in effect today; Cursor Q3 close on track
ipoTracker.process: SpaceX priced at $135/share on June 11, raising $75 billion at a $1.75 trillion valuation in the largest IPO ever recorded. Shares peaked at $225.64 on June 16 and are at $157.54 as of July 3. Grok 4.5 (1.5 trillion parameters, V9 architecture) is in private beta at Tesla and SpaceX. A 2T parameter successor is in final training expected late July. Grok 5 (6-10T parameters) is in development on Colossus 2. The $60B Cursor acquisition is on track for Q3 2026 close. Both Colossus facilities are committed to third parties.
ipoTracker.confidence: Confirmed; public company as of June 12, 2026
ipoTracker.note: Tesla AI cap (Grok exempt) takes effect today. Grok 5 development on Colossus 2 is a key strategic signal. Both Colossus facilities commercialized raises questions about xAI's own dedicated training compute.

latestArticle.title: Grok 4.5 private beta confirmed at SpaceX and Tesla; Grok 5 development begins

ipoTracker.status: CBRS public (Nasdaq, May 2026); Q1 2026 revenue $193.4M (+92% YoY); GPT-5.6 Sol Cerebras deployment expected this month at 750 tok/sec; $20B+ OpenAI compute deal active
ipoTracker.process: Cerebras completed its Nasdaq IPO in May 2026 under ticker CBRS. Q1 2026 results (released June 23) massively beat estimates: $193.4M revenue vs. $56.65M expected (+92% YoY). Major deals: multi-year OpenAI compute deal (750MW, $20B+ valued, option to $30B) and a multi-year AWS partnership pairing Cerebras CS-3 with Trainium 3 for inference disaggregation. OpenAI is deploying GPT-5.6 Sol on Cerebras wafer-scale hardware this month at up to 750 tokens per second.
ipoTracker.note: GPT-5.6 Sol Cerebras deployment this month is the key near-term positive catalyst.

latestArticle.title: Cerebras Systems Announces Strong First Quarter 2026 Results

ipoTracker.stage: Public company — IPO completed March 2025; joined Nasdaq-100 June 22, 2026
ipoTracker.status: CRWV at ~$82-83 (markets reopen today after holiday); recovery from Meta cloud fears; Nasdaq-100 member; Evercore Outperform/$150; take-or-pay through 2032; backlog $99.4B
ipoTracker.process: CoreWeave completed its Nasdaq IPO in March 2025 (priced at $40/share) and joined the Nasdaq-100 on June 22, 2026 (15 months post-IPO), replacing Charter Communications. CRWV dropped 14% on July 1 following Bloomberg's Meta Compute cloud report and is at approximately $82-83 as markets reopen. Evercore ISI reiterated Outperform/$150, citing the $21B Meta take-or-pay contract through 2032. Revenue backlog: $99.4B (+50% QoQ). FY2026 revenue guidance: $12-13 billion.
ipoTracker.note: Nasdaq-100 membership adds index fund buying pressure. NVIDIA Kyber NVL144 delay (July 6) does not affect existing Vera Rubin NVL72 deployments.

latestArticle.title: CoreWeave to Join Nasdaq-100 Index

ipoTracker.status: IPO at $42/share; raised $2.1B; $15B market cap; ticker HFCE on Nasdaq; open-source AI infrastructure bellwether
ipoTracker.process: Hugging Face completed its Nasdaq IPO on June 9, 2026, pricing at $42 per share and raising $2.1 billion at a $15 billion market cap under ticker HFCE. The IPO is widely seen as an open-source AI watershed — the first major public listing of a company whose primary assets are open-source model repositories, training infrastructure, and developer community.
ipoTracker.confidence: Confirmed; public company as of June 9, 2026
ipoTracker.note: Track as an indicator of investor appetite for open-source AI business models.

latestArticle.title: Inside the Hugging Face IPO: What It Means for Open-Source AI in 2026

ipoTracker.stage: Watchlist candidate — CEO explicitly IPO-bound; H2 2026 or 2027 S-1 expected
ipoTracker.status: No filing confirmed; CEO Ali Ghodsi called 2026 'terrible year to go public' but is explicitly IPO-bound; $5.4B ARR at 65% YoY growth; $165-175B valuation target
ipoTracker.process: Databricks CEO Ali Ghodsi has shifted to an explicitly IPO-bound posture targeting 2027 or H2 2026 at $165-175 billion valuation. Despite calling 2026 'a terrible year to go public' in early June (citing the SpaceX/Anthropic/OpenAI trifecta absorbing $200B+ in IPO capital), reporting indicates an S-1 filing is still expected in H2 2026. The company has $5.4 billion ARR growing 65% YoY with positive free cash flow. SpaceXAI announced native Grok availability on Databricks Agent Bricks at the 2026 Data + AI Summit (June 18).
ipoTracker.confidence: H2 2026 or 2027 S-1 expected per reporting; no filing confirmed
ipoTracker.note: Strong fundamentals support the $165-175B valuation target. Watch for S-1 filing announcement.

latestArticle.title: IPO-bound Databricks reportedly eyes $175B valuation after hitting $5.4B revenue run rate

ipoTracker.stage: Watchlist candidate — no primary-source filing confirmed

ipoTracker.process: Some IPO tracker sites claim Scale AI filed an S-1 in March 2026, but no matching SEC filing, Scale AI announcement, or credible primary-source confirmation has been found as of July 6, 2026. Scale raised more than $14 billion in June 2025 and is unlikely to pursue a near-term IPO given its recent large private financing.
ipoTracker.confidence: Low until verified by primary source

ipoTracker.stage: High-growth private company — 2028 IPO target confirmed; Apple acquisition discussions unresolved
ipoTracker.status: 2028 IPO reaffirmed; Windows PC launch imminent; Perplexity for Gov live; $500M ARR; 100M MAU; $20B valuation; Apple discussed acquisition at $20B
ipoTracker.process: CEO Aravind Srinivas publicly stated a preference for an independent 2028 IPO. ARR reached $500 million (+335% YoY) with 100M+ monthly active users and a $20 billion valuation from the May 2026 round. Personal Computer GA on Mac since May 7; Windows version with hybrid local-cloud inference orchestrator targeting July 2026 (imminent). Perplexity for Gov launched alongside the GSA with FedRAMP compliance underway. Apple executives have reportedly discussed acquiring Perplexity at its $20B valuation — no move has been made.
ipoTracker.confidence: No filing; 2028 IPO is stated preference; Apple acquisition reported but not confirmed
ipoTracker.note: Windows Personal Computer this month is the key near-term product catalyst. 2028 IPO timeline is firm per CEO.

latestArticle.title: Apple reportedly discussed acquiring Perplexity AI

partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed; Colossus 1 serving Anthropic at ~$1.25B/month through May 2029; Colossus 2 serving Reflection AI since July 1; full Claude traffic running after Fable 5 restoration
partnershipTracker.terms: SpaceXAI provides Anthropic access to Colossus 1 at approximately $1.25 billion per month through May 2029 (90-day exit clause). Colossus 1 houses approximately 220,000+ NVIDIA GPUs across 300MW. Colossus 2 began serving Reflection AI on July 1 at $150M/month ($6.3B through 2029), meaning both Colossus facilities are committed to third parties. Fable 5 and Mythos 5 export controls were lifted July 1, restoring full global Claude access and resumed normal workload flow to Colossus 1.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed relationship; monthly payment figure from reporting, not company-confirmed
partnershipTracker.note: No structural change on July 6. Anthropic's China enforcement crackdown does not affect the Colossus 1 compute relationship.

latestArticle.title: SpaceX signs $6.3 billion compute deal with Reflection AI for Colossus data center

partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed; $100B+/10-year, 5GW Trainium; Project Rainier active at ~500K chips; ASCI classified cloud expands federal Claude distribution; jailbreak framework active
partnershipTracker.terms: The April 2026 expanded agreement commits more than $100 billion over 10 years, securing up to 5GW of Trainium2/3/4 capacity. Project Rainier runs nearly half a million Trainium2 chips for Claude workloads, with 1M+ chips expected by year end. Amazon invested $5B in Anthropic's $65B Series H. AWS Secret Cloud for Industry (ASCI) expands federal Claude distribution. Microsoft, Anthropic, AWS, and Google co-developed a jailbreak severity scoring framework announced July 4.
partnershipTracker.note: ASCI classified cloud creates a new federal distribution channel for Claude. No new contract changes on July 6.

latestArticle.title: Anthropic and Amazon expand collaboration for up to 5 gigawatts of new compute

partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed; ~3.5GW TPU capacity starting 2027; Apollo/Blackstone $35B AI XPV Platform operational; AVGO down ~26% from high — does not affect TPU supply terms
partnershipTracker.terms: Anthropic's agreement with Google and Broadcom secures approximately 3.5 gigawatts of next-generation TPU capacity expected to start coming online in 2027. Google has invested up to $40 billion in Anthropic. The Apollo/Blackstone $35B AI XPV Platform (operational since June 9) facilitates Anthropic's 1+ gigawatt compute expansion at Fluidstack-based sites beginning mid-2026 using Broadcom XPUs. NVIDIA Kyber NVL144 delay does not affect this TPU relationship.
partnershipTracker.note: No structural change on July 6. TPU capacity comes online in 2027.

latestArticle.title: Anthropic expands partnership with Google and Broadcom for multiple gigawatts of next-generation compute

partnershipTracker.type: Cloud, model distribution, investment, and GPU optimization
partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed; Claude in Microsoft Foundry GA on GB300 since June 29; Frontier Co. day 5 adds enterprise channel; Maia 200 negotiations ongoing; NVIDIA Kyber delay does not affect GB300 supply
partnershipTracker.terms: Anthropic has committed to purchase $30 billion of Azure compute capacity. Claude models in Microsoft Foundry on NVIDIA GB300 Blackwell Ultra GPUs are generally available as of June 29. Microsoft plans a 40% GB300 capacity expansion by September 2026. Microsoft Frontier Co. ($2.5B, 6,000 embedded engineers) adds a new enterprise deployment channel for Claude. Microsoft and Anthropic remain in active negotiations to deploy Claude on Maia 200 custom AI accelerators. The NVIDIA Kyber NVL144 delay does not affect the GB300-based GA deployment or existing Vera Rubin NVL72 shipments.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed; Claude in Foundry GA milestone achieved June 29; Maia 200 talks reported but not finalized
partnershipTracker.note: NVIDIA Kyber NVL144 delay is notable context for this relationship — future rack-scale expansions beyond NVL72 are pushed to 2028. No immediate impact on current GA infrastructure.

latestArticle.title: Microsoft, NVIDIA and Anthropic deepen AI collaboration with Claude general availability

partnershipTracker.status: Early-stage; Samsung participated in Anthropic's $65B Series H; Anthropic hired former OpenAI Jalapeño chip engineer in June; 2nm chip talks at design-exploration stage
partnershipTracker.terms: Anthropic held early-stage talks with Samsung Electronics as a potential manufacturing partner for a custom AI inference accelerator, targeting Samsung's 2-nanometer foundry process and advanced packaging facilities. Anthropic hired Clive Chan (former OpenAI custom chip engineer who helped build the Broadcom-designed Jalapeño ASIC) in early June 2026. Samsung participated in Anthropic's $65B Series H alongside SK Hynix and Micron. Anthropic has not yet determined chip function, performance targets, or power envelope. The company may not proceed.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Reported by The Information and Bloomberg; confirmed early-stage exploration; no formal agreement
partnershipTracker.note: Samsung is also reportedly in early-stage talks with Meta for custom MTIA chips. This remains exploration-stage — not a committed contract.

latestArticle.title: Samsung reportedly in talks to make 2nm AI chips for Anthropic and Meta

partnershipTracker.type: Cloud, product distribution, and IP licensing
partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed amended partnership; Copilot Cowork billing day 6; Frontier Co. deepens enterprise channel; GPT-5.6 restricted; broader access expected July 10-17
partnershipTracker.terms: Microsoft remains OpenAI's primary cloud partner under a non-exclusive IP license through 2032 (amended April 27, 2026). GPT-5.6 Sol, Terra, and Luna remain restricted to approximately 20 organizations; broader access expected July 10-17. Copilot Cowork billing at $0.01/Copilot Credit is in its sixth day. Microsoft Frontier Co. ($2.5B, 6,000 embedded engineers) adds enterprise professional services deployment capacity for OpenAI products. Microsoft is evaluating DeepSeek V4 for some Cowork workloads to reduce API costs.
partnershipTracker.note: July 10-17 GPT-5.6 broader access is the next model availability milestone for this relationship.

latestArticle.title: Microsoft commits $2.5 billion and 6,000 employees to new AI implementation unit

partnershipTracker.type: Cloud, model distribution, and strategic infrastructure partnership
partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed; GPT-5.5 and Codex GA on Bedrock; GPT-5.6 expected on Bedrock July 10-17; ASCI and ICAMF expand federal market; $50B Amazon Stargate investment
partnershipTracker.terms: OpenAI's frontier models (GPT-5.5, GPT-5.4) and Codex are generally available on AWS Bedrock since June 1. GPT-5.6 Sol is expected on Bedrock in the July 10-17 window. Amazon invested $50 billion in the March 2026 Stargate funding round. OpenAI holds a 2GW Trainium capacity contract. AWS Summit DC ICAMF and ASCI expand the federal market for OpenAI workloads on Bedrock.
partnershipTracker.note: GPT-5.6 July 10-17 broader release is the next key Bedrock milestone for this relationship.

latestArticle.title: OpenAI frontier models and Codex are now available on AWS

partnershipTracker.type: GPU infrastructure and strategic supplier relationship
partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed; 10GW partnership; H2 2026 first-gigawatt Vera Rubin on track; Kyber NVL144 delay pushes next rack upgrade to 2028
partnershipTracker.terms: NVIDIA agreed to deploy at least 10GW of NVIDIA systems for OpenAI infrastructure, with the first gigawatt targeted for H2 2026 on Vera Rubin. NVIDIA invested $30 billion in Stargate in March 2026. Vera Rubin production shipments began in July 2026 to AWS, Google Cloud, Microsoft Azure, OCI, and CoreWeave. The NVIDIA Kyber NVL144 delay (reported July 6) pushes the next-generation rack upgrade beyond 2027 into 2028, but does not affect the existing Vera Rubin NVL72 commitment.
partnershipTracker.note: Kyber NVL144 delay is notable context — the H2 2026 first-gigawatt milestone depends on NVL72, which is unaffected. Future rack-scale expansions are pushed.

latestArticle.title: NVIDIA Kyber NVL144 delayed to 2028 — Vera Rubin NVL72 shipments unaffected

partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed; 6GW partnership beginning with 1GW MI450 in H2 2026; MI450 samples shipping; Oracle 50K unit MI450 cluster Q3; NVIDIA Kyber delay may improve AMD competitive timing; AMD Advancing AI 2026 July 22-23
partnershipTracker.terms: OpenAI and AMD agreed on a multi-generation 6GW GPU deployment beginning with an initial MI450 rollout of approximately 1GW in H2 2026. AMD CEO Lisa Su confirmed MI450 samples are shipping to OpenAI. Oracle's 50,000-unit MI450 deployment in Q3 2026 adds the first publicly available MI450-based AI supercluster. AMD Advancing AI 2026 (July 22-23) will formally launch MI450. The NVIDIA Kyber NVL144 delay removes a competing next-generation rack system from the 2026-2027 window, potentially improving AMD Helios rack timing.
partnershipTracker.note: AMD Advancing AI 2026 on July 22-23 is the next major milestone. NVIDIA Kyber delay is a competitive tailwind for AMD Helios rack market positioning.

latestArticle.title: AMD and OpenAI announce strategic partnership to deploy 6 gigawatts of AMD GPUs

partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed; Stargate Abilene TX flagship (1.2 GW, Oracle Cloud) fully operational; $50B Amazon and $30B NVIDIA Stargate investments committed; Jalapeño ASIC targeting H2 2026 Stargate deployment
partnershipTracker.terms: OpenAI's Stargate compute platform spans Oracle, SoftBank, CoreWeave, and related infrastructure partners. The Abilene, TX flagship (1.2 GW on Oracle Cloud) is fully operational. Amazon invested $50 billion and NVIDIA invested $30 billion in the March 2026 Stargate funding round. Jalapeño — OpenAI's first custom ASIC co-developed with Broadcom — targets H2 2026 Stargate deployment with 1.3 GW compute committed for 2027.
partnershipTracker.note: Jalapeño H2 2026 Stargate deployment is the next major structural milestone. Treat Stargate as a living infrastructure network.

latestArticle.title: OpenAI and Broadcom unveil Jalapeño — first custom AI inference chip

partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed; $21B take-or-pay through December 2032; Meta Compute cloud does not affect existing contract; Digitimes confirms 2026 supply commitments unchanged
partnershipTracker.terms: CoreWeave confirmed Meta expanded the relationship to provide AI cloud capacity through December 2032 for approximately $21 billion, including initial NVIDIA Vera Rubin deployments. The contract is take-or-pay — Meta cannot redirect spend without financial penalty. Meta's announcement of Meta Compute cloud (July 1) raised investor concerns about future CoreWeave utilization, but Digitimes reported July 3 that server supply chain contacts see no change to Meta's 2026 shipment and revenue commitments.
partnershipTracker.note: Take-or-pay protection through 2032 is the key fact distinguishing near-term revenue security from longer-term Meta Compute pricing competition risk.

latestArticle.title: CoreWeave and Meta Announce $21 Billion Expanded AI Infrastructure Agreement

partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed; multi-year agreement; Vera Rubin NVL72 clusters at full utilization following Fable 5 restoration July 1; Kyber delay does not affect NVL72
partnershipTracker.terms: CoreWeave and Anthropic entered a multi-year agreement for Claude workloads at production scale, with infrastructure rolling out in phases beginning in 2026. Financial terms were not disclosed. Following the July 1 Fable 5 restoration, full Anthropic Claude inference traffic is running on CoreWeave's Vera Rubin NVL72 clusters. The NVIDIA Kyber NVL144 delay (July 6) does not affect the existing NVL72-based deployment.
partnershipTracker.note: Full utilization restored after July 1 Fable 5 restoration. No new contract terms on July 6.

latestArticle.title: CoreWeave Announces Multi-Year Agreement With Anthropic

partnershipTracker.status: Rebuilding after Fable 5 ban; full Claude restored July 1; Pentagon dispute court docs (released July 4) reveal unresolved autonomous weapons policy disagreement; Maven Smart System retains DoD designation
partnershipTracker.terms: Palantir and Anthropic bring Claude to U.S. government intelligence and defense users inside Palantir products running on AWS infrastructure. The Fable 5 ban (June 12-July 1) disrupted the pipeline, with the Pentagon reportedly migrating two-thirds of its AI workflows off Anthropic models. Court documents released July 4 reveal a dispute between Dario Amodei and Pentagon Undersecretary Emil Michael over autonomous weapons and domestic surveillance limits. Full Fable 5 access was restored July 1. Maven Smart System retains its DoD program-of-record designation.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed partnership; Pentagon dispute details from court documents reported July 4
partnershipTracker.note: Pentagon dispute court docs remain an unresolved risk factor. The partnership is rebuilding after the 19-day Fable 5 ban. ASCI classified cloud creates a new federal AI distribution channel that could partially restore DoD pipeline.

latestArticle.title: Pentagon contract dispute documents released — Amodei vs. Emil Michael

partnershipTracker.type: AI model integration and distribution agreement
partnershipTracker.status: Strained; Apple defaulted to Google Gemini as primary Siri AI; OpenAI retained outside counsel over perceived breach; iOS 27 Extensions framework disabled pending EU DMA; Apple evaluating Mistral and discussed acquiring Perplexity or Mistral
partnershipTracker.terms: Apple and OpenAI signed an integration agreement for ChatGPT in iOS/Siri. At WWDC June 8, Apple announced iOS 27 would feature a fully rebuilt Siri powered by Google Gemini rather than ChatGPT. The iOS 27 Extensions framework theoretically allows user choice among ChatGPT, Claude, and Gemini inside Siri, but remains disabled pending EU Digital Markets Act regulatory clarity. OpenAI retained outside counsel and is weighing a formal breach-of-contract notice. Apple's exploration of Mistral and Perplexity as alternatives reinforces its multi-provider hedging strategy.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Original agreement confirmed; breach-of-contract dispute reported, not adjudicated
partnershipTracker.note: No resolution on July 6. Both sides prefer out-of-court resolution. Apple's Mistral/Perplexity acquisition discussions would reduce dependence on both OpenAI and Google.

latestArticle.title: Apple reportedly considered buying Mistral AI and Perplexity

partnershipTracker.type: GPU supply and cloud AI infrastructure partnership
partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed ongoing supplier relationship; MI300X powers Azure AI; MI450 evaluation for Azure underway; NVIDIA Kyber delay may accelerate MI450 Azure adoption timeline
partnershipTracker.terms: AMD is a GPU supplier to Microsoft Azure for AI workloads, with MI300X and MI450 (when available) powering Azure AI compute capacity alongside NVIDIA hardware. Microsoft is evaluating MI450 for Azure data center deployments in H2 2026. The NVIDIA Kyber NVL144 delay (July 6) may accelerate Microsoft's MI450 Azure evaluation timeline, as NVL144-based rack upgrades are now pushed to 2028.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed ongoing relationship; MI450 Azure evaluation reported but not announced
partnershipTracker.note: NVIDIA Kyber NVL144 delay is a potential competitive tailwind for AMD's Azure footprint expansion. AMD Advancing AI 2026 (July 22-23) may clarify Azure MI450 deployment terms.

latestArticle.title: AMD Advancing AI 2026 — July 22-23 at Moscone Center

partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed; $60B/6GW multi-generation agreement; MI450 Helios rack shipments targeting H2 2026; Samsung also in talks for custom Meta chips
partnershipTracker.terms: AMD and Meta signed a $60 billion, 6GW multi-generation GPU supply agreement covering MI450 and successor generations. AMD CEO Lisa Su confirmed MI450 samples are shipping to Meta. Helios rack-scale systems (3 AI exaflops per rack) with MI450 GPUs are targeted for H2 2026 shipments to Meta. Meta's $125-145 billion 2026 capex plan supports both the AMD GPU partnership and Meta's custom MTIA accelerator program. Samsung is also reportedly in early-stage talks to produce custom chips for Meta alongside its Anthropic chip discussions.
partnershipTracker.note: MI450 Helios rack H2 2026 shipments to Meta are the next major delivery milestone. Meta Compute cloud adds a monetization layer on top of the Meta-AMD infrastructure partnership.

latestArticle.title: Samsung reportedly in talks to make 2nm AI chips for Anthropic and Meta

partnershipTracker.type: Strategic equity investment and primary GPU supply relationship
partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed; NVIDIA holds ~11% equity stake; CoreWeave is primary commercial Vera Rubin NVL72 deployer; Kyber NVL144 delay does not affect NVL72 supply
partnershipTracker.terms: NVIDIA holds approximately 11% equity stake in CoreWeave. CoreWeave purchases NVIDIA hardware at scale — $31 billion capex floor for 2026 alone — and is among the first cloud providers to deploy Vera Rubin NVL72 racks in production. The NVIDIA Kyber NVL144 delay (reported July 6) does not affect the NVL72 deployments already underway at CoreWeave. NVIDIA's revenue-sharing AI factory model (announced July 2) may deepen the economic alignment beyond one-time hardware sales.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed; equity stake and supply relationship confirmed by both companies
partnershipTracker.note: Kyber NVL144 delay is neutral-to-positive for CoreWeave in the near term — NVL72 remains the primary shipping product and CoreWeave's 2026 capex plan is already committed.

latestArticle.title: NVIDIA Kyber NVL144 delayed to 2028 — Vera Rubin NVL72 shipments unaffected
